reason: Enabling a certmap section in /etc/sssd/sssd.conf is a straightforward authentication configuration change. It does not involve partitioning, bootloader, or FIPS settings, and follows typical STIG hardening patterns. While an incorrect mapping could affect authentication, the risk can be mitigated with backup and testing, so it is considered safe to remediate.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential authentication failure if mapping is incorrect; Service restart may temporarily affect login services; Misconfiguration could lock out users relying on certificate authentication
